top_signals.title: Aluminum Extends Slump as China Data Adds to Downside Pressure
top_signals.summary: Aluminum dropped to its lowest since March after weaker-than-expected economic data from China dragged on metals, while investors assessed lingering questions over the execution of a peace deal between Iran and the US.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Australia Grapples with Demographic Shifts: Falling Fertility, Immigration
top_signals.summary: While the anti-immigration One Nation party is gaining traction in opinion polls, Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has resisted calls for deeper cuts to migrant intake. That's as Australia faces its own demographic pressures with the fertility rate at a record low. Roger Wilkins of the Melbourne Institute discusses the broader challenges and implications for Australia's future on 'Bloomberg: The Asia Trade.' (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: US government watchdog urges FDIC coordinate on crypto oversight
top_signals.summary: The US Government Accountability Office says that regulators, including the FDIC, lack an “ongoing coordination mechanism for addressing blockchain risks.”
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: SpaceX, United Airlines, Roku & more - CNBC
top_signals.summary: Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: SpaceX, United Airlines, Roku & more  CNBC
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: UK to ban social media access for children under 16
top_signals.summary: The ban will apply to all “user-to-user platforms, whose purpose is to enable social interaction and which allow users to post material, alongside algorithms,” according to a press release from the government’s Department for Science, Innovation and Technology.
